Title: Visually impaired teen scores 8As in SPM
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: A visually impaired teenager from Keningau scored 8As in the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination despite struggling with blurred vision since childhood.
Eighteen-year-old Alexzander Dinus said a nerve problem caused him to see multiple images, making it difficult to follow lessons in class.
"Sometimes I see three images. If the teacher writes on the board in front of the class, it is difficult for me to see.
"Every day, I study by asking for help from teachers and friends. Sometimes, even when the teacher writes something down, I still cannot see it clearly.
"But because our teachers are kind, they patiently teach me until I understand," he said in a statement issued after the 2025 SPM and Sijil Tinggi Agama Malaysia Excellence Awards Ceremony here yesterday.
The eldest of five siblings said his success had motivated him to pursue his dream of becoming an educator and that he hoped to further his studies at the Teacher Education Institute.
"My advice to others in the same situation as me is never give up. We may have shortcomings, but that does not mean we are weak," he said.
Meanwhile, his father, Panis Guntalib, said the family had accepted Alexzander's condition after trying various treatments over the years.
"Now we accept fate, and what matters is that he continues learning and remains confident in himself.
"To parents who have special children like this, continue giving them support and encouragement," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RBC lifts S&P 500 year-end target to 7,900 on AI optimism
Article:
NEW YORK: RBC Capital Markets raised its year-end target for the S&P 500 to 7,900 from 7,750 on Friday, citing resilient earnings growth and continued strength in artificial intelligence-linked sectors.
The Canadian brokerage's new target implies a 7.7 per cent upside from the benchmark index's Thursday close of 7,335.66.
US equities have rallied to hit record highs in recent weeks as enthusiasm around AI-related investment and expectations of solid profit growth continue to support investor appetite.
The S&P 500 posted its biggest monthly percentage gain since November 2020 last month.
RBC's bullish stance follows similar moves by major Wall Street brokerages such as J.P. Morgan and Barclays, which raised their targets for the index last month, citing easing geopolitical risks and improving earnings momentum.
RBC said positive earnings revisions, driven by technology and AI-linked firms, alongside strong demand for AI infrastructure have supported valuations. It added that US companies have remained resilient to higher costs and geopolitical risks, keeping leadership concentrated in large-cap growth stocks.
Strategists at RBC also said the rally has continued despite a challenging macro backdrop marked by sticky inflation, uncertainty over the timing of US rate cuts, and lingering geopolitical risks.
Separately, among sectors, RBC downgraded US healthcare stocks to "market weight" from "overweight" due to earnings revisions, fund outflows and policy uncertainty despite still-attractive valuations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Palm flat as traders wait for key data; on track for second weekly loss
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian palm oil futures were largely flat on Friday, as traders preferred to wait for next week's demand and supply data from the palm oil board.
The benchmark palm oil contract for July delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange was unchanged at RM4,541 per ton by the midday break.
The contract has declined 1.03 per cent so far this week and is on track for a second weekly fall.
The market is largely in consolidation mode, awaiting the release of the Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) data, said Paramalingam Supramaniam, director at brokerage Pelindung Bestari.
MPOB is expected to release its monthly demand and supply data on Monday.
"The market is pegging end-stocks to remain within market expectations of around 2.3 to 2.5 million metric tons, and any significant deviation from that range will likely dictate the market's next directional move," he said.
"We are also noticing that the price action is closely tracking crude oil movements, with little regard for the fundamentals."
Keeping prices in check were the diverging prices of rival edible oils and crude oil, which palm tracks.
Dalian's most-active soyoil contract fell 1.14 per cent, while its palm oil contract shed 0.87 per cent. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade were up 0.65 per cent.
Palm oil competes with other edible oils for a share of the global vegetable oils market. Oil prices rose about one per cent after renewed fighting broke out between the US and Iran, threatening a shaky ceasefire.
Stronger crude oil futures make palm a more attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.
The ringgit, palm's currency of trade, weakened 0.31 per cent against the dollar, making the commodity slightly cheaper for buyers holding foreign currencies.
Palm oil may bounce to RM4,563 per metric ton, as it is stabilising around a support at RM4,482, Reuters technical analyst Wang Tao said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesian authorities rushing to rescue 20 hikers after Mount Dukono eruption
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesian authorities are rushing to rescue 20 hikers after the eruption of Mount Dukono on Halmahera island on Friday, a local rescue agency official told Reuters.
Mount Dukono, located in North Maluku province, erupted on Friday at 7.41am local time (1041 GMT Thursday), spewing volcanic ash as high as 10km (6.21 miles) into the sky, the country's volcanology agency said.
The agency maintained the alert status at the mountain at its third-highest level, agency head Lana Saria said in a statement.
Iwan Ramdani, the head of the local rescue agency, told Reuters that they have deployed dozens of personnel, including police, to search for the 20 hikers trapped by the eruption.
Nine of the hikers are Singaporean and the rest are Indonesians, Iwan said.
Authorities warned residents to refrain from any activities within 4km (2.5 miles) of the crater.
The volcanology agency also warned of the risks of volcanic mudflow when it rains.
There are not yet any reports of flight disruptions caused by the eruption.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia's FX reserves fall to US$146.2 billion at end-April, lowest in nearly two years
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia's forex reserves dropped by US$2 billion in April to US$146.2 billion, the lowest level in nearly two years, due to intervention in the FX market to defend the rupiah amid rising uncertainties, Bank Indonesia said on Friday.
The central bank said the level of reserves was supported by tax and service revenues, and by a government global bond sale last month.
BI said reserves were equal to 5.8 months' worth of imports.
Indonesia raised US$1.1 billion in a Samurai bond sale in April.
The rupiah has repeatedly hit record lows against the dollar this year on investor concerns about the economic and fiscal impact of the government's pro-growth policies and the war in the Middle East.
Rating agencies Moody's and Fitch have downgraded their credit rating outlooks on Indonesia to negative.
The rupiah hit a record low of 17,445 per dollar on Tuesday.
BI has said it has sufficient reserves to make the strong market interventions needed to stabilise the rupiah.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RTS Link to be nation's busiest gateway; 90pct complete ahead of Dec 31 launch
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor Baru–Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link is set to become Malaysia's busiest land entry point, with operations focused on high-tech security and efficiency ahead of its Dec 31 launch.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the 4km rail link is expected to handle up to 10,000 passengers per hour in each direction, with a travel time of just six minutes.
"Security, immigration control, threat detection, and operational efficiency must be at the highest level from day one. There will be no compromise," he said after inspecting the Bukit Chagar Immigration, Customs, Quarantine, and Security (ICQS) Complex today.
As of April 30, the project is 90 per cent complete. The government is now intensifying preparations for security, communications, and manpower.
Key security installations include 18 baggage scanners, 22 body scanners, and 10 walkthrough scanners using millimetre wave technology.
These are integrated with Auto Threat Detection systems to allow for precise screening without disrupting passenger flow.
Saifuddin said the system would strike a balance between seamless movement and stringent enforcement, with all passenger movements recorded, analysed and monitored in real time to prevent smuggling, illegal entry and other cross-border crimes.
On immigration, the RTS Link will deploy 220 e-gates, shifting away from manual passport checks. These will be integrated with the new MyNIISe system, which replaces the legacy MyIMMs platform. System testing is scheduled for June.
Saifuddin acknowledged that while system transitions may face minor glitches, mitigation measures are in place.
"There is no such thing as a perfect system during transition. What matters is our commitment to minimise disruptions and ensure the new system becomes a game changer in addressing congestion," he said.
On manpower, a total of 794 personnel positions have been approved, including 640 under the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA), 47 from Customs, and 107 police officers.
A batch of 478 recruits will begin training in Segamat on June 22.
The RTS Link features a co-located checkpoint model, allowing Malaysian and Singaporean officers to operate within shared facilities.
Saifuddin noted that enabling laws on the Malaysian side are currently being gazetted, while Singapore is in the process of enacting its own legislation.
He said ongoing bilateral technical discussions are focused on harmonising standard operating procedures to ensure smooth, secure and coordinated cross-border operations.
"The level of coordination, discipline and trust between both countries is at its best. This is not just about infrastructure, but about legal and regulatory alignment," he said.
Separately, Saifuddin clarified that while possessing a MyKad remains compulsory for Malaysians aged 12 and above, there is no requirement for immediate replacement when the upgraded version is introduced on June 1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang records RM63.96bil in approved investments for 2025
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang strengthened its status as one of Malaysia's main economic growth engines after recording RM63.96 billion in approved investments in 2025, Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib said today.
Ramli said the state continued to position itself not only as the nation's industrial heartbeat, but also as a regional hub for high-impact technology and investments.
Speaking at the opening of the first meeting of the fourth term of the 15th Penang State Legislative Assembly, he said Penang sustained its strong investment momentum last year, according to figures from the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida).
"The manufacturing sector remained the main driver with investments amounting to about RM22.38 billion, led by the electrical and electronics (E&E) industry.
"The services sector meanwhile, recorded about RM41.22 billion, with significant contributions from the information and communications technology (ICT) subsector," he said in his address.
Ramli said the investment composition reflected balanced confidence among both domestic and foreign investors in Penang's economic prospects.
Domestic investments totalled RM38.85 billion, while foreign investments stood at RM25.10 billion.
In tandem with the robust investment performance, Penang also posted strong trade figures in 2025.
Citing data from the Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade), Ramli said the state's total trade surged 17.9 per cent year-on-year to RM956.37 billion.
Exports reached RM573.91 billion while imports amounted to RM382.46 billion.
"The performance enabled Penang to retain its position as one of the country's biggest export contributors, accounting for about 35.7 per cent of Malaysia's total exports," he added.
Ramli said the achievements underscored the resilience and competitiveness of Penang's investment ecosystem, which continued to command the confidence of both local and international investors.
"These achievements further reinforce Penang's position as one of the country's primary economic growth engines and a key pillar in national economic development," he said.
In his speech, Ramli also touched on Penang's efforts to strengthen its digital economy, high-skilled workforce and sustainable infrastructure as part of its long-term development agenda.
He also spoke on major infrastructure projects, including the Mutiara Line Light Rail Transit (LRT), Juru-Sungai Dua project and Penang International Airport expansion, which would strengthen mobility, connectivity and economic growth in the state.
He added that Penang was also reinforcing water sustainability through new water resource laws and strategic initiatives, including a memorandum of understanding between the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) and a consortium involving Perak State Development Corporation and Gamuda Berhad for treated water supply from Sungai Perak.
Ramli said Penang continued to gain international recognition as a tourism and investment destination, while the state also remained committed to environmental sustainability, women empowerment, food security, youth development and smart city governance, including achieving 100 per cent 4G coverage and 91.9 per cent 5G coverage as of March this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No compromise on those exploiting 3R issues, says Tengku Mahkota Pahang
Article:
KUANTAN: The Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah, has issued a stern warning against those attempting to threaten harmony in the state by manipulating sensitive issues.
Opening the 15th State Legislative Assembly sitting at Wisma Sri Pahang today, he said there would be no compromise for those who exploit race, religion, and the royal institution (3R).
Tengku Hassanal said all quarters, regardless of background, must act responsibly in all matters and uphold the values of respect, tolerance, and the rule of law.
"I want 3R issues to stop, and there will be no compromise for those who exploit them. Any action that incites hatred between races and religions is absolutely unacceptable.
"I view the incident involving the desecration of the Quran at UMPSA (Universiti Malaysia Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah) seriously," he said in his speech.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/regional/2026/02/1385163/updated-university-student-arrested-after-apologising-police-station
Tengku Hassanal said that ahead of the upcoming 16th General Election, all parties are advised not to play up issues that could disrupt unity and to practise mature politics.
He said the opposition must play its role as a constructive check and balance, while the government must ensure that the welfare of the people is continuously protected without leaving anyone behind.
On a separate matter, Tengku Hassanal said that any issues related to the construction of places of worship, whether on government or private land, must fully comply with existing laws and regulations.
"Harmony must be preserved and must not become a cause of disputes between races and religions.
"No one should act based on emotion, and also no one is above the law when dealing with matters that may threaten peace and stability in the state," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia in negative territory at midday on uncertain Middle East developments
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia maintained its downward momentum at midday, in line with the weaker performance across regional markets as sentiment was dragged by renewed developments surrounding the United States (US)-Iran conflict.
It was reported that the US and Iran exchanged fire in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, although US president Donald Trump said the ceasefire remained in place.
At 12.30 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) fell 7.01 points, or 0.40 per cent, to 1,751.84 from Thursday's close of 1,758.85.
The benchmark index, which opened 0.78 points lower at 1,758.07, moved between 1,749.82 and 1,760.19 during the morning trading session.
Market breadth was negative with losers outpacing gainers 699 to 356, while 486 counters were unchanged, 1,111 untraded and nine suspended.
Turnover stood at 1.93 billion units, valued at RM1.36 billion.
Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said the local bourse tracked the choppy Wall Street session overnight, while Brent crude climbed back above US$100 per barrel.
"As such, the FBM KLCI is expected to remain volatile and headline-driven, particularly on developments surrounding the US-Iran conflict and potential diplomatic efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
"Sentiment is likely to stay cautious amid renewed clashes between the US and Iran, coupled with Washington still awaiting Tehran's response to proposals aimed at de-escalation and restoring shipping flows through the Strait of Hormuz," it said in a note.
Despite near-term volatility, the KLCI has maintained a strong uptrend, supported by positive first-quarter 2026 earnings expectations across banks, utilities, energy and healthcare, alongside sustained domestic liquidity and a constructive macro backdrop.
The bank said Malaysia's status as a net energy exporter also offers relative resilience against higher oil prices versus regional peers.
"Going forward, market focus will shift to earnings guidance and macroeconomic signals as investors balance resilient fundamentals against rising geopolitical risks, higher energy costs, supply chain pressures and potential growth headwinds," it added.
Technically, key support is seen at 1,710-1,730, while resistance stands at 1,771-1,789, said HLIB.
Among the heavyweights, Malayan Banking Bhd and Tenaga Nasional Bhd slipped six sen each to RM11.20 and RM14.74, respectively.
Public Bank Bhd shed three sen to RM4.86, CIMB Group Holdings Bhd gave up five sen to RM7.99, and IHH Healthcare Bhd rose one sen to RM8.89.
Among the most active counters, Top Glove Corp Bhd added 1.5 sen to 7.5 sen, ACE Market debutant Inspace Creation Bhd inched up one sen to 26 sen, Hartalega Holdings Bhd gained six sen to RM1.24, and Tanco Holdings Bhd improved two sen to RM1.67.
Among the top gainers, Hong Leong Industries Bhd surged RM1.14 to RM19.34, Petronas Dagangan Bhd soared 20 sen to RM20.76, Pentamaster Corp Bhd went up 18 sen to RM4.56, and TIME dotCom Bhd gained 16 sen to RM6.30.
Among the top losers, Nestle (Malaysia) Bhd dropped RM2.20 to RM101.90, UMS Integration Ltd slipped 29 sen to RM7.08, Batu Kawan Bhd trimmed 26 sen to RM21.14, and Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd shed 24 sen to RM21.06.
On the index board, the FBM Emas Index fell 41.95 points to 12,938.78, the FBM Top 100 Index reduced 43.26 points to 12,783.48, the FBM Emas Shariah Index slid 21.35 points to 12,841.78, the FBM Mid 70 Index declined 28.34 points to 18,428.75, and the FBM ACE Index slipped 58.42 points to 4,607.32.
By sector, the Financial Services Index gave up 87.30 points to 20,386.13, the Energy Index eased 1.97 points to 809.89, and the Plantation Index fell 73.28 points to 8,864.49, while the Industrial Products and Services Index was marginally higher by 0.73 of a point at 195.15.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bartender charged with drunk driving in fatal crash involving young mother
Article:
AYER KEROH: A 23-year-old bartender was charged at the magistrate's court today with driving under the influence of alcohol and causing the death of a woman motorcyclist on Tuesday.
S. Darshan pleaded not guilty after the charge was read before magistrate Uthman Abd Ghani.
He was accused of driving with a blood alcohol level above the legal limit, resulting in a collision that killed Nur Aleya Natasha Roslan, 21.
The incident occurred in Jalan Ujong Pasir, in front of the Pasir Mutiara Condominium, at 2.30am on May 5.
The charge, under Section 44(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Amendment 2020), carries a prison term of 10 to 15 years and a fine of between RM50,000 and RM100,000 upon conviction.
The accused also faces a 10-year disqualification from holding a driving licence starting from the date of conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Syauqi Mardia Baharudin proposed bail of RM10,000 and requested the court to impound the accused's driving licence.
National Legal Aid Foundation (YBGK) lawyer Muhamad Mawardi Zainudin, representing Darshan, pleaded for lower bail, as his client earns RM2,200 a month and supports his 58-year-old father.
Uthman allowed bail at RM6,000 with one surety and set June 10 for mention.
It was previously reported that the victim, a mother of a two-year-old, died at the scene from severe head and leg injuries after her motorcycle was struck by a Perodua Myvi.
Melaka Tengah district police chief Assistant Commissioner Christopher Patit said the car reportedly veered into the opposite lane before colliding with the victim's motorcycle.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Matrix Concepts bags five property awards at StarProperty Awards 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd has clinched five honours at the StarProperty Real Estate Developer Awards 2026, marking another milestone as the company celebrates its 30th anniversary year.
The awards recognised the developer's township projects in Negri Sembilan and Johor.
Matrix Concepts group executive director Kelvin Lee Chin Chuan said the recognitions reflect the team's continued dedication and the trust placed in the company by its homeowners and stakeholders over the past three decades.
"We are honoured to receive these recognitions at the StarProperty Awards 2026.
"They highlight the commitment our teams demonstrate every day, and they speak to the trust that has supported our growth over the years," he said in a statement.
Lee added that the recognition comes at a meaningful point as the company enters a new phase of progress, further strengthening its resolve to continue creating environments that support families and contribute to long-term community wellbeing.
"As we move into our next chapter, we remain focused on delivering people-first developments that continue to shape lives and landscapes across the nation," he said.
Matrix Concepts said that it celebrates a landmark moment this year as the awards mark its 10th consecutive year of recognition at the StarProperty Awards since 2017.
"It is a proud achievement that reflects not only the quality of its developments, but also the commitment, dedication and community values that continue to define the company's work," it said.
Among this year's awards was The Cornerstone Award (Best Landed Development), Negri Sembilan presented to Suria Heights, a neighbourhood known for its balanced and contemporary interpretation of family living.
This was followed by The Family-Friendly Award (Best Family Centric Development, Landed), Negri Sembilan awarded to Bayu Sutera, one of Bandar Sri Sendayan's most thoughtfully planned neighbourhoods.
Meanwhile, Bandar Seri Impian in Kluang was honoured with The Neighbourhood Award (Best Comprehensive Township, 500–2,000 acres).
In addition, Matrix Concepts was recognised in the StarProperty Readers' Choice Awards, being named among the Top 3 Most Preferred Developers.
Complementing these achievements, Matrix Concepts was also ranked fifth place in the All-Star Award under the Listed Company category.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Immigration raid at Cyberjaya foreigner hub nets 13
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department detained 13 foreigners during an integrated operation at Menara Paragon in Cyberjaya, described by authorities as a hub for foreigners.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the raid was carried out at 9pm on May 6 following public complaints and a week of intelligence-gathering.
A total of 83 Immigration officers and 15 officers from the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry took part in the operation.
"The operation focused on food premises, kiosks, sales centres and business premises suspected of employing or harbouring foreigners without valid permits," he said in a statement today.
Zakaria said 16 premises were inspected, involving checks on 105 foreigners and 89 Malaysians.
He said the 13 foreigners detained, aged between 26 and 35, were believed to have committed various offences under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and Immigration Regulations 1963.
"They were from Sudan, India, Pakistan, Nepal, Myanmar and Indonesia.
"We also issued a notice to a Malaysian to assist in the investigation," he said.
Zakaria said all detainees had been sent to the Putrajaya Immigration Depot.
"Action will also be taken against anyone employing or harbouring illegal immigrants," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fireman charged with sexual assault of 14-year-old student
Article:
IPOH: A 28-year-old fireman was charged at the Sessions Court today with seven counts of physical sexual assault and possession of a pornographic image of a 14-year-old student.
Muhammad Aidilafifi Muhammad pleaded not guilty to all charges before judge Jean Sharmila Jesudason.
He was accused of assaulting the boarding school student in Parit for sexual purposes by hugging her, kissing her cheeks and lips, and groping and biting her breasts.
The offences were allegedly committed at a mall parking lot in Seri Iskandar, inside and outside the school's hostel area, and in front of a restaurant between early February and May 1.
The charges fall under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, punishable under the same section read together with Section 16(1). This carries a prison term of up to 20 years and whipping.
Aidilafifi, who is also a school Fire and Rescue Cadet unit trainer, was also charged with possessing a pornographic picture of the victim.
The content was allegedly found on his mobile phone at the Perak Tengah district police headquarters at 5pm on May 3.
The possession charge, under Section 10 of the same Act, carries up to five years in jail, a fine of up to RM10,000, or both.
Deputy public prosecutor Nor Arfahanim Azureen Arfanuddin requested that no bail be granted. However, she suggested RM80,000 for all counts, should the court allow it.
In mitigation, lawyer Ahmad Shamil Azad Abdul Hamid said his client had cooperated fully with the police and had served as a fireman for over four years.
"He is a civil servant earning RM2,500 a month and supports his parents and siblings. I plead for a bail of RM5,000," he said.
The judge set bail at RM9,000 for all charges and ordered the accused to report to a police station monthly. He is also prohibited from contacting the victim, her family, or any witnesses.
The court set June 23 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Manufacturing sales rise 5.3pct to RM173.1bil in March 2026: Statistics Department
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The manufacturing sector posted sales value of RM173.1 billion in March 2026, an increase of 5.3 per cent year-on-year (YoY), said the Statistics Department.
In a statement today, chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the growth in sales value within the manufacturing sector was mainly contributed by the electrical and electronics (E&E) products sub-sector, which rose 13.3 per cent in March 2026.
"This was further supported by the food, beverages and tobacco sub-sector; as well as non-metallic mineral products, basic metal and fabricated metal products sub-sector, which increased by 7.8 per cent and 3.7 per cent respectively," he said.
On a month-on-month basis, sales value rose by 8.7 per cent, from RM159.2 billion recorded in February 2026.
Meanwhile, Mohd Uzir said sales value growth in the export-oriented industries, which account for 71.7 per cent of total sales, expanded by 6.5 per cent in March 2026, led by the increase in the manufacture of computers, electronics and optical products at 15.2 per cent.
Besides that, he said the manufacture of vegetable and animal oils and fats also registered an increment at 10.2 per cent, while the manufacture of machinery and equipment grew 2.7 per cent.
As compared to the sales value in the preceding month, the export-oriented industries surged by 10.7 per cent.
Mohd Uzir said the domestic-oriented industries increased 2.5 per cent in March 2026, after registering 2.7 per cent in the previous month. mainly supported by the growth in the manufacture of food processing products industry at 5.0 per cent in March 2026, as well as in the manufacture of basic metals and the manufacture of other non-metallic mineral products (3.2 per cent).
On employment, he said a total of 2.4 million employees were recorded in this sector during March 2026, augmented by 1.1 per cent, mainly driven by food, beverages and tobacco (2.1 per cent), followed by the E&E products and non-metallic mineral products, basic metal and fabricated metal products, which all recorded 1.4 per cent.
The number of employees in this sector decreased 0.1 per cent on a month-on-month comparison.
He added that the salaries and wages paid in the manufacturing sector also posted an expansion of 2.2 per cent, amounting to RM8.56 billion in March 2026.
As compared to the amount recorded in the prior month, the salaries and wages paid declined 0.2 per cent. Subsequently, the sales value per employee registered at RM71,705 (4.2 per cent), while the average salaries and wages per employee was RM3,547, an increase at 1.1 per cent YoY.
For the first quarter of 2026, the manufacturing sector recorded sales value of RM 501.5 billion for a growth of 5.5 per cent (fourth quarter 2025: 5.8 per cent).
The number of employees increased by 1.1 per cent to 2.4 million persons, while salaries and wages increased by 2.3 per cent to RM25.9 billion. The sales value per employee stood at RM207,779, an increase of 4.4 per cent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UAE countering Iranian air attack after Trump says ceasefire still in effect
Article:
WASHINGTON/CAIRO: US ally the United Arab Emirates said its air defences were engaging missile and drone threats from Iran early on Friday in a further test of the shaky, month-long ceasefire between the US and Iran.
There were few details immediately available about the latest attack on the UAE, which came a day after the US and Iran exchanged fire around the Strait of Hormuz, and as Washington awaited a response from Teheran to its proposal to end the conflict.
Iran has often targeted the UAE and other Gulf countries that host US bases since the war began on February 28.
President Donald Trump said on Thursday three US Navy destroyers were attacked as they moved through the strait, a conduit for around a fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas flows that Iran has all but closed since the conflict started.
"Three World Class American Destroyers just transited, very successfully, out of the Strait of Hormuz, under fire. There was no damage done to the three Destroyers, but great damage done to the Iranian attackers," Trump wrote on Truth Social.
Trump later told reporters the ceasefire was still in effect and sought to play down the exchange.
"They trifled with us today. We blew them away," Trump said in Washington.
Iran's top joint military command accused the US of violating the ceasefire by targeting an Iranian oil tanker and another ship, and of carrying out air attacks on civilian areas on Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz and the nearby coastal areas of Bandar Khamir and Sirik on the mainland.
The military said it responded by attacking US military vessels east of the strait and south of the port of Chabahar.
A spokesperson for Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters said the Iranian strikes inflicted "significant damage", but US Central Command said none of its assets were hit.
Iran's Press TV later reported that, following several hours of fire, "the situation on Iranian islands and coastal cities by the Strait of Hormuz is back to normal now."
The two sides have occasionally exchanged gunfire since the ceasefire took effect on April 7, with Iran hitting targets in Gulf countries including the UAE.
Oil prices rose in early trade in Asia on Friday, with Brent crude jumping above US$100 a barrel after the latest clashes between the US and Iran.
Trump urges negotiated end to war
Trump suggested ongoing talks with Teheran remained on track despite Thursday's hostilities, telling reporters, "We're negotiating with the Iranians."
Before the latest strikes, the US had floated a proposal that would formally end the conflict but did not address key US demands that Iran suspend its nuclear work and reopen the strait.
Teheran said it had not yet reached a decision on the emerging plan.
Even so, Trump said Teheran had acknowledged his demand that Iran could never get a nuclear weapon, a prohibition he said was spelled out in the US proposal.
"There's zero chance. And they know that, and they've agreed to that. Let's see if they are willing to sign it," Trump said.
Asked when any deal might be reached, Trump said, "It might not happen, but it could happen any day. I believe they want to deal more than I do."
The war has tested Trump's relationship with his US base of supporters, after he had campaigned against involving the United States in foreign wars and promised to bring down fuel prices.
Average US gasoline prices have climbed more than 40 per cent since late February, rising by about US$1.20 a gallon to more than US$4, according to data from the American Automobile Association, as disruptions to oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz pushed crude oil prices higher.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: LBS wins four major accolades, strengthening position as design-led developer
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: LBS Bina Group Bhd has clinched four major accolades at a recent property awards event, reinforcing the group's growing reputation as a leading design-led property developer and reinforcing its long-term strategic direction towards upscale, sustainable and community-centric developments.
The property developer received the Foreign Touch Award for Centrum Iris, the Excellence in the Business Estate Award for Alam Perdana Industrial Park, and the Excellence in the Starter Home Award for KITA Sejati at the StarProperty Real Estate Developer Awards 2026.
The group was also named among the top 10 property developers in Malaysia, further strengthening its standing as a trusted and progressive player in the local property industry.
Lucas Lim, deputy chief executive officer of LBS Bina Group Bhd, received the 'Top 10 High Performing Companies Award' from Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming.
In a statement, LBS said these recognitions reflect the group's unwavering brand promise of "Building Tomorrow Together", which goes beyond property development to shaping meaningful lifestyles, thriving business ecosystems, and sustainable communities.
The recognition for Centrum Iris reinforces LBS' design-led approach, where thoughtful architecture and curated living spaces elevate homebuyers' expectations and enhance modern lifestyles.
Meanwhile, the award for Alam Perdana Industrial Park underscores the group's commitment to supporting the nation's economic ecosystem by developing business environments that enable industries, entrepreneurs, and communities to grow and thrive together.
KITA Sejati's recognition as an outstanding starter home development demonstrates LBS' people-first values, reaffirming its commitment to making quality homeownership accessible for young families and first-time buyers.
Being named among the top 10 property developers in Malaysia further strengthens LBS' position as a trusted and forward-thinking developer, consistently delivering value-driven developments that meet the evolving needs of Malaysians.
LBS group executive chairman Tan Sri Dr Lim Hock San said the group was honoured to receive the prestigious recognitions, describing them as a testament to the group's dedication to excellence and innovation.
"These achievements reaffirm LBS' commitment to excellence, innovation, and community-building. The awards reflect our brand promise of 'Building Tomorrow Together', with design, functionality, and people at the heart of every development," Lim said.
Lim added that the group remains focused on advancing its strategic roadmap towards the upscale segment while strengthening its position as a design-led developer.
As LBS celebrates these milestones, he said the group will continue delivering sustainable, aspirational, and value-driven spaces for modern homebuyers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested 'with weapon' near ex-prince Andrew's home
Article:
LONDON: UK police said Thursday they had arrested a man wielding a weapon who "was behaving in an intimidating manner" close to the home of former prince Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor in eastern England.
Norfolk police confirmed they had been called to an incident late Wednesday near the Sandringham Estate "and the man was arrested on suspicion of a public order offence and possession of an offensive weapon."
UK media reported that a man wearing a balaclava had allegedly threatened Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor when he was out walking his dogs in the eastern Sandringham area.
The man had been sitting in his car when he saw Mountbatten-Windsor, and allegedly got out of the vehicle and headed towards the ex-prince shouting at him, the Daily Telegraph said.
Andrew had been accompanied by a member of his private security, and fled to his own car, driving away at speed, the newspaper added.
It was the first media outlet to report on the incident.
Norfolk police said in a statement that "officers were called to Wolferton shortly after 7:30pm yesterday."
The man "was taken to King's Lynn police investigation centre for questioning and remains in custody."
Andrew, the second of the late queen Elizabeth II's three sons and brother to Charles III, was arrested in mid-February amid new revelations stemming from his ties to the late billionaire US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
He was questioned for hours at a police station on suspicion of misconduct in public office during his decade-long role as a UK trade envoy.
Mountbatten-Windsor has denied any wrongdoing, and has not been charged, but remains under police caution.
Andrew was stripped of his royal titles in October by the king amid the growing scandal around Epstein, who died in prison in 2019.
He has also been ousted from his previous home at Royal Lodge in Windsor, and has moved to the king's private Sandringham estate.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang denies misuse of RM500,000 in fishermen voucher programme
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government has denied allegations of misappropriation involving a RM500,000 fishermen's voucher redemption programme following a police report lodged by eight suppliers over delayed payments.
State Rural Development, Agrotechnology, Food Security and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman Datuk Rashidi Zinol said the delay was due to technical and procedural issues involving several agencies, rather than any misuse of funds.
"There is no misappropriation, and I deny the allegation," he said on the sidelines of the state legislative assembly sitting today.
Under the programme, fishermen can redeem vouchers for equipment, including nets, from participating suppliers who then claim payment from the state government.
In their police report, the traders claimed the state government had committed to settling payments within two weeks of the redemption and verification process. However, they said payments have been outstanding for over a year, straining their businesses.
The traders said they had participated in a similar programme in 2019, where payments were made on schedule without issue.
Rashidi said RM293,000 of the RM500,000 allocation had already been paid to the companies.
"The remaining RM207,000 involved the Fisheries Development Authority of Malaysia (LKIM) and the State Development Office (BPEN).
"There were technical issues in the process. The funds were returned to BPEN recently and today the authorities are processing payments to the companies involved," he said.
Rashidi said six of the eight companies would receive payment first as they had submitted complete documentation. The remaining two must still fulfil several licensing and documentation requirements.
"I have appointed a special officer, including someone involved during the previous exco administration, to assist the companies in completing the necessary documents. Most of the documentation issues have already been resolved," he said.
He added that the state government initially intended to complete payments last week, but several procedures had to be regularised first.
He said he expects payments for companies with complete documentation to be finalised by next week.
Rashidi said the state government had no issue with any police probe into the matter as no misappropriation had occurred.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Kuala Lumpur Declaration key to strengthening Asean resilience
Article:
CEBU: The Kuala Lumpur Declaration, adopted by Asean last year, must serve as a key foundation in strengthening the region's economic resilience, energy security and supply chain stability amid ongoing global geopolitical uncertainty, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Anwar said Asean should build on the momentum of the declaration, which reaffirms collective commitments to strategic autonomy, economic resilience and sustainable growth.
In his address at the 48th Asean Summit plenary session here today, he said the declaration also recognises that energy security, food resilience, supply chain stability and stronger regional connectivity have now become strategic priorities rather than mere sectoral issues.
"We must build on the momentum of the Kuala Lumpur Declaration adopted last year, which reaffirms our shared commitment to strategic autonomy, economic resilience and sustainable growth.
"The declaration recognises that energy security, food resilience, supply chain stability and stronger regional connectivity are now strategic priorities, not merely sectoral concerns," he said.
Anwar also said Asean must continue to uphold the bloc's centrality through practical cooperation to ensure the region remains open, inclusive and resilient amid growing geopolitical fragmentation.
Malaysia, he added, supports efforts to accelerate the implementation of the Asean Petroleum Security Agreement (APSA) framework to strengthen regional energy security amid declining domestic oil and gas production in the region.
"We must also diversify fossil fuel sources and reduce over-reliance on vulnerable supply chains.
"We must recognise that strategic cooperation with trusted partners and regional groupings can strengthen Asean's long-term energy security."
The prime minister also called on Asean member states to leverage cooperation with the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), including broader Asean-GCC-China linkages, to build a more resilient energy system.
At the same time, he said the Asean Power Grid (APG) remains a key component in the region's energy transition and long-term energy security, requiring greater investment to accelerate cross-border connectivity and low-carbon growth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 20 people missing after volcano erupts in east Indonesia
Article:
Jakarta: Twenty people, nine of them from Singapore, were reported missing Friday after Mount Dukono on Indonesia's eastern Halmahera island erupted, spewing a smoke cloud some 10 kilometres (6.2 miles) into the air, a rescue official told AFP.
"Our team is on its way. It is not yet confirmed if there are injured people. Based on reports, there are about 20 people being searched for," search and rescue official Iwan Ramdani said by telephone.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three South Koreans in court over RM39.6mil Genting Highlands crypto-kidnapping
Article:
RAUB: Three South Korean men were produced at the Sessions Court today to face charges for the high-profile kidnapping of a compatriot in Genting Highlands last month, involving a cryptocurrency ransom worth RM39.66 million.
However, the charges against the suspects, aged between 28 and 40, were not read as they did not understand Malay or English.
Pahang prosecution director Sharizat Ismail and deputy public prosecutor Fathin Nadhirah Kasim requested a new mention date to secure a Korean interpreter and did not offer bail.
Judge Noor Zaihan Mohamad Ali set May 18 for mention and ordered the three accused to be remanded without bail.
The three are being charged under Section 3(1) of the Kidnapping Act 1961, which carries a prison sentence of between 30 and 40 years and whipping upon conviction.
Police recently crippled the South Korean-led syndicate after rescuing a man who was abducted while travelling from Genting Highlands to Kuala Lumpur on April 17.
The victim was reportedly followed before his vehicle was intercepted. He was held at a homestay in Melaka for four days.
The kidnappers allegedly demanded a ransom of 10 million Tether (USDT) from the victim's family. The family reportedly paid 3 million USDT, equivalent to approximately RM11.9 million, to secure his release.
Investigations revealed the victim is wanted by South Korean authorities for suspected involvement in illegal gambling and is on the Interpol list.
Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar had previously said that the victim had been in Malaysia since 2016 to evade authorities in his home country.
His passport had also been cancelled by the South Korean Embassy.
Following the arrests, police seized a replica pistol, a Richard Mille luxury watch, three passports, four mobile phones, and RM112,000 in cash and foreign currency.
Authorities also managed to freeze and recover 2.46 million USDT of the ransom, worth approximately RM11.5 million.
Investigations found that two of the suspects entered Malaysia legally in March, with their permits expiring in June. Police believe the motive was purely financial gain and that the suspects did not know the victim.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang must not be a 'mere observer' amid global uncertainty, says TYT
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang cannot afford to remain a mere observer amid growing global economic and geopolitical uncertainties, says Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib.
Opening the first meeting of the fourth term of the 15th Penang State Legislative Assembly today, Ramli said the state must take proactive measures to strengthen its resilience and internal capabilities.
"In confronting this increasingly challenging reality, Penang cannot merely be an observer.
"This sovereign state must continue to act proactively in strengthening its internal capacity and resilience by emphasising energy efficiency, enhancing food security and ensuring disciplined and forward-looking fiscal management," he said in his address.
Ramli said prolonged tensions in several strategic regions, including in the Middle East, had triggered chain reactions affecting global market stability, particularly in the energy sector and supply chains.
He said Malaysia, as an open economy, was not spared from the impact due to its significant dependence on the region, including for oil, liquefied natural gas (LNG) and critical materials such as fertiliser, helium and sulphur.
"Malaysia is not exempt from the spillover effects of these developments due to our dependence on the region, including for about 61 per cent of oil imports, 30 per cent of LNG imports and nearly 40 per cent of crude oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz," he added.
Ramli, however, said the nation's economic fundamentals remained strong and resilient due to decisive measures taken by the federal government under Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Citing projections and official data from the Statistics Department, he said the country's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth for this year was forecasted at between 4.0 and 5.0 per cent, while inflation was expected to remain moderate at between 1.5 and 2.5 per cent.
He also expressed confidence that the federal government would continue monitoring global developments closely and implement appropriate measures to safeguard the people's welfare, ensure a sufficient supply of essential goods and sustain economic growth.
Ramli also reminded elected representatives to uphold responsible and factual communication in debates and public discourse.
"In this climate of uncertainty, any statement not based on facts or which is speculative in nature will only create unnecessary anxiety among the people.
"We must remind ourselves that inappropriate remarks can bring negative consequences," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tengku Hassanal: Federal govt must restore Pahang's rights over sand exports
Article:
KUANTAN: The Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah, has expressed deep disappointment over the federal government's decision to ban river sand exports, saying the move has hindered state revenue growth.
He said Pahang, which has the longest river in Peninsular Malaysia, accumulates large sand deposits that must be dredged to maintain water flow and prevent flooding.
"We previously generated tens of millions in revenue through sand exports to other countries. However, the federal government no longer allows it, even though land and sand resources are state rights.
"I also want the federal government to grant greater authority and flexibility to the state government in implementing development projects," he said when opening the 15th State Legislative Assembly sitting at Wisma Sri Pahang today.
Tengku Hassanal said he hoped companies and contractors from Pahang would be given priority for projects within the state.
"The appointment of G7-grade contractors can be made from anywhere in the country. However, I want priority given to qualified and competent companies from Pahang.
"I stress that the federal government must review this matter and restore Pahang's rights.
"We should not reach a point where Pahang is forced to consider legal action against the federal government, and I ask the State Legal Adviser to study the possible action regarding this matter," he said.
Tengku Hassanal said the federal government should not overlook Pahang's contributions as the largest state in Peninsular Malaysia.
"Pahang's sacrifices cannot simply be measured in terms of tax contributions to the federal government, but are highly significant in maintaining environmental balance, especially in Peninsular Malaysia.
"The state has preserved vast forest areas, natural flora and fauna, mountain ranges that serve as the ecological backbone, water catchment areas, and biodiversity resources for the country," he said.
He emphasised that, as a sovereign state, Pahang does not require interference from other states in any legal or administrative matters.
"Any misconduct that occurs in Pahang must be fairly adjudicated in court. There should be no interference from any party in upholding justice," he said.
.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] No rush to replace MyKad, says Home Minister
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Malaysians are required by law to possess a MyKad, but will not be compelled to immediately upgrade to the new version when it is introduced on June 1.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the rollout of the enhanced MyKad, featuring improved security and technology, does not require existing cardholders to replace their identity cards right away.
He stressed that while possessing a MyKad is mandatory for citizens aged 12 and above, the law does not mandate a blanket or simultaneous switch to the upgraded card.
"Possessing a MyKad is mandatory under the law. However, when we introduce the new MyKad on June 1 with enhanced security features, it does not mean all existing holders must immediately replace their cards.
"We encourage the public to upgrade, but it is not compulsory to do so right away," he said.
Saifuddin said this at a press conference after inspecting the progress of the Johor Baru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link project at the Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex at Bukit Cagar station here today.
He said more than 20 million Malaysians currently hold MyKad, and advised the public against overcrowding National Registration Department (NRD) offices nationwide.
"There is no need to rush on June 1. Take your time to make the change," he said.
Malaysia is expected to roll out a
new-look passport and MyKad
from June 1 as part of efforts to strengthen the security of national identification documents.
The move marks a significant step in reinforcing document integrity, as the Malaysian passport remains among the world's most trusted travel documents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two teens remanded for another week over brutal murder of college student
Article:
KOTA BARU: Two teenagers linked to the brutal murder of a college student found with 61 stab wounds in Ketereh last week have had their remand extended by seven days.
Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid issued the extension today, effective until next Thursday, to facilitate investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
The suspects, both 19, were previously remanded for a week following their arrest.
Last Friday, the body of a 19-year-old student was discovered in Kampung Padang Simah, Ketereh, with 61 stab wounds across her body.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the victim was found without identification, but was later confirmed through National Registration Department fingerprint records.
Police believe the victim was killed elsewhere before her body was dumped at the site.
Four individuals were initially detained in connection with the case, including the parents of one suspect. The elderly couple was released on police bail on Wednesday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Motorists praised for helping stranded woman push motorcycle in Kerinchi [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two motorists who stopped to help a woman push her motorcycle along a busy road near KL Gateway Mall have won praise from Malaysians online.
A 43-second video shared on social media showed the woman struggling alone to push her motorcycle along the road towards Kerinchi, believed to be near the mall, during a light drizzle.
According to the person who recorded the incident, she was waiting for her husband near the Universiti LRT station when she spotted the woman from a pedestrian bridge.
"She looked exhausted. I was not sure whether the motorcycle had broken down or run out of fuel, but she was clearly struggling on her own.
"She would walk while pushing it, then occasionally sit on the motorcycle and continue trying to move it. With the light rain falling, I just kept hoping someone would stop to help her," the caption read.
Moments later, two men were seen getting out of their car to help push the motorcycle to safety.
The act of kindness quickly drew praise from netizens, many describing the men as "heroes", commending their willingness to help a stranger in need.
"Proud moment. There are still many good people around us.
"Big applause to both of you. May all your affairs be eased," the uploader wrote.
Many social media users echoed the sentiment, saying such simple acts of compassion were a reminder that kindness still exists in everyday life.
⁠
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's biodiesel moment
Article:
FOR years, Malaysia's biodiesel policy has behaved a little like a plantation road after rain - passable, promising but never entirely smooth.
At times, the journey advanced with confidence.
At other times, it slowed behind the familiar potholes of infrastructure cost, subsidy anxiety, depot readiness, engine concerns and that old argument between the dinner plate and the diesel tank.
Now the engine has started again.
With B15 biodiesel production scheduled to begin from June 1 through 19 licensed plants, Malaysia is being invited to revisit a question that has never quite gone away: should palm biodiesel remain a cautious blending programme, or become a more deliberate pillar of national energy security?
The move is expected to begin with B15, progress to B20, and possibly reach B50 within two to three years.
The question matters because the world outside is not exactly behaving politely.
Crude oil prices remain exposed to conflict at Hormuz, shipping anxieties and geopolitical surprises.
Diesel imports can be expensive. Fuel subsidies are heavy. And when energy markets sneeze, national budgets tend to catch more than a cold.
Against that backdrop, Malaysia has something many countries would envy: a home-grown oil crop, existing biodiesel plants, spare capacity, and a value chain that supports both rural incomes and industrial resilience.
Yet having the asset is one thing. Using it wisely is another.
The Numbers Behind B15
For clarity, the figures matter. Malaysia's total biodiesel production capacity is about 2.4 million tonnes a year.
In 2025, actual production stood at around 1.3 million tonnes. Of this, the existing B10 blending programme absorbed about one million tonnes.
Moving from B10 to B15 would require another 400,000 tonnes, bringing total B15 blending demand to about 1.4 million tonnes.
In other words, Malaysia is not being asked to invent a new industry overnight.
The capacity exists. The plants exist. The palm oil exists.
The harder question is whether production, logistics, blending infrastructure, pricing and policy coordination can move in step.
As always, the palm oil train can move - provided the tracks, signals and station masters are properly aligned.
The cost comparison also explains why biodiesel has returned to the policy table.
CNA indicated industry estimates that diesel was close to RM6.20 per litre, while biodiesel was about RM4.50 per litre.
That spread makes biodiesel increasingly attractive when conventional diesel prices are vulnerable to global disruptions.
But arithmetic on paper must still travel through pipes, depots, engines, pumps and public confidence.
Policy may be announced at the rostrum, but implementation is tested at the terminal.
The Depot Is Not a Detail
Malaysia currently mandates B10 for motorists, meaning 10 per cent biodiesel and 90 per cent petroleum diesel.
Scaling up to B15, B20 or B30 is therefore not only a matter of asking producers to produce more.
It requires blending readiness, terminal upgrades, storage systems, quality control, logistics and confidence from vehicle users.
Here lies one of the less glamorous but very real constraints.
Malaysia has biodiesel blending depots, most designed around B10 capacity.
Upgrading this infrastructure could take one to two years, while moving towards B30 may require more than RM600 million in terminal and infrastructure spending.
This is why biodiesel cannot be sold merely as a slogan.
A mandate must eventually pass through steel, pumps, tanks, fittings and invoices. In policy, enthusiasm is free. Infrastructure is not.
Yet infrastructure should not become an excuse for permanent hesitation.
If Malaysia wishes to treat biodiesel seriously, blending infrastructure must also be treated as strategic energy infrastructure.
Otherwise, we will keep rediscovering the same problem every time crude oil turns rude.
A Crop That Can Do More
Malaysia produces roughly 20 million tonnes of palm oil annually, but only about 1.3 million tonnes is used for biodiesel.
That suggests room to think more boldly about how palm oil fits into the country's energy and industrial strategy.
For too long, palm oil has often been asked to defend itself only as food. Yet it is more than that.
It is a rural income generator, export earner, industrial feedstock, oleochemical base, biomass source and, increasingly, a bioenergy instrument.
To recognise palm biodiesel as strategic is not to abandon food security.
It is to understand that a national crop of this scale should not be trapped inside a narrow conversation.
The palm oil value chain must keep asking how every tonne, residue and side stream can create greater value.
That wider bioenergy story is already emerging.
Palm oil mill effluent, used cooking oil, palm fatty acid distillate, palm acid oil and other residues are becoming part of the broader biofuel conversation.
Biodiesel may be today's policy focus, but tomorrow's opportunity may also include sustainable aviation fuel, hydrogenated vegetable oil, bio-naphtha and other renewable pathways.
In that sense, B15 should not be viewed as the final destination. It may be better understood as a necessary gear change.
Indonesia's Bolder Script
Across the straits, Indonesia has moved more aggressively.
Its biodiesel programme is not merely green policy.
It is import-substitution policy, farmer policy, industrial policy and energy-security policy wearing the same batik shirt.
Malaysia has often been more cautious, partly because its fuel import dependency and fiscal structure differ from Indonesia's.
CNA reported that Indonesia imported 35.2 per cent of its crude oil in 2023, compared with Malaysia's 15.6 per cent, which partly explains why Indonesia has pushed harder on biodiesel.
Malaysia does not need to copy Indonesia wholesale.
Different countries have different economics, policy tools and political pressures. But Malaysia should still learn from the seriousness with which Indonesia treats biodiesel as a strategic instrument.
The question is not whether Malaysia should become Indonesia.
The question is whether Malaysia should become more Malaysian in using what Malaysia already has.
The Table Still Has a Say
Still, biodiesel is never only about the tank. It also touches the table.
The food-versus-fuel debate often sounds moral, but it is mostly economics wearing a moral tie.
Higher biodiesel demand can support CPO prices and growers' incomes.
But it can also squeeze refiners, food manufacturers, consumers and the treasury if edible oil prices rise too sharply.
That is why biodiesel policy must be honest. It can reduce fossil-fuel reliance, strengthen domestic demand, support rural income and lower emissions.
But pushed too far, too fast, it may intensify food-price pressures.
The wiser question is not whether biodiesel is good or bad. It is how far, how fast, with whose money, and under what safeguards.
Biology Still Votes
There is another inconvenient truth: demand can be mandated, but supply must be grown.
Malaysia's palm oil production has struggled to move sustainably beyond the 20 million tonne.
Ageing palms, slow replanting, labour constraints, climate stress and rising input costs all limit how far ambition can outrun agronomy.
A blending mandate can create demand, but it cannot make old palms young, or replace replanting, mechanisation, fertiliser discipline, field supervision and smallholder renewal.
In short, biodiesel can support palm oil prices. But if Malaysia wants a stronger biodiesel programme, it must also strengthen the upstream sector.
Between Promise and Prudence
Where does that leave Malaysia? Between promise and prudence.
Biodiesel is not a magic potion. It cannot abolish diesel, end fuel-price pressures or turn old palms young again.
But neither is it a decorative green label to be dusted off only during crises. It is a strategic instrument - useful, imperfect, and too important for slogans.
Used wisely, it can reduce import dependence, support growers, strengthen downstream industry, improve energy resilience and give palm oil a stronger role in a lower-carbon world.
Used carelessly, it can strain consumers, distort markets and invite subsidy headaches.
The B15 move should therefore be welcomed, but not romanticised.
The industry must be ready, depots upgraded, standards clear, the public informed, and the fiscal burden honest.
Malaysia has a golden crop. It should use it strategically, but not mistake strategy for slogan.
In the end, biodiesel is best seen not as a crisis remedy, but as a long-term national instrument - part energy security, part rural economy, part climate response and part palm oil value creation.
The tank, the table and the treasury will continue to argue. Perhaps they should.
A debate this consequential deserves clear thinking, steady execution and enough humility to remember that in palm oil, as in policy, the fruit bunch may be heavy - but the consequences are heavier.
*Joseph Tek has over 30 years of experience in the plantation industry, with a strong background in oil palm R&D, executive leadership and industry advocacy through MEOA and MPOA. The views expressed in this article are his own.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aidiladha moon sighting set for May 17 at 29 locations
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The sighting of the new moon of Zulhijjah to determine the date of Aidiladha will take place on Sunday at 29 locations nationwide.
The Office of the Keeper of the Rulers' Seal said the official date for Aidiladha would be announced the same night via radio and television broadcasts.
"The Conference of Rulers has agreed that the determination of the date for Aidiladha will be based on the rukyah (moon sighting) and hisab (astronomical calculations) methods," it said in a statement.
The 29 locations include Baitul Hilal Sultan Ismail Building in Pontian, Johor; the Al-Khawarizmi Astronomy Complex in Melaka; the Negri Sembilan Observatory; and Baitul Hilal sites in Bukit Melawati and Bukit Jugra, Selangor.
Other observation points include the Selangor Observatory in Sabak Bernam; Baitul Hilal Pantai Pasir Panjang in Perak; and the Sheikh Tahir Astronomy Centre in Penang.
In Kedah, sightings will be conducted at Kampung Pulau Sayak, Pemandangan Indah on Langkawi Island, and Menara Alor Setar.
Terengganu sites include Bukit Besar, Bukit Geliga, Pulau Perhentian, and the Kusza Observatory, while Kelantan locations include Bukit Peraksi, Bukit Kampong Tembeling, and Menara Bangunan SEDC.
In Sarawak, the moon will be sighted at the falak centres in Miri and Bintulu, as well as Teluk Bandung in Kuching. In Sabah, the sighting will take place at the Al-Biruni Observatory in Putatan.
Pahang locations include Bukit Tanjong Batu, Gunung Brinchang, and Menara 188. Additional sites are the Universiti Malaysia Sabah Tower in Labuan, Kuala Lumpur Tower, the Putrajaya International Convention Centre, and Masjid Al-Hussain in Kuala Perlis.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man found dead in attic following Sungai Petani house fire
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: A man was found dead following a fire at a house in Lorong Astana, Bandar Sri Astana, early today.
A Kedah Fire and Rescue Department spokesman said an emergency call was received at 4.51am, after which 22 personnel from the Sungai Petani and Aman Jaya stations were dispatched to the scene.
Operations commander Mohd Zaki Senik said the team arrived at 5.07am and found the single-storey house engulfed in flames.
The fire destroyed about 80 per cent of the structure and is believed to have started at the front of the house.
"A Perodua Alza and four motorcycles were also destroyed," he said.
Zaki said the victim's body was found in the attic at 5.23am. He was confirmed dead at the scene by medical personnel from the Health Ministry.
A man in his 50s suffered from breathing difficulties during the incident and was sent to Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital for treatment.
Zaki added that the cause of the fire and the total losses are still under investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Elderly man dies after Manjung home invasion
Article:
MANJUNG: An elderly man was killed while his wife sustained serious injuries after they were attacked during a robbery at their home in Jalan Melayu, Ayer Tawar, early yesterday.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Hasbullah Abd Rahman said police received a call regarding the incident at 5.30am.
Initial investigations found that the elderly couple were assaulted by a man who entered their house and attacked them with a hard object.
"The suspect fled with two mobile phones and a purse belonging to the victims before escaping from the scene," he said in a statement today.
Hasbullah said neighbours who heard the commotion rushed to help the victims and took them to Seri Manjung Hospital for treatment.
"However, the 84-year-old male victim was pronounced dead while receiving treatment, while his wife remains seriously injured.
"A post-mortem examination will be conducted to determine the exact cause of death," he said.
Following intelligence gathering and investigations, police arrested a 27-year-old man at 7.45pm on the same day.
"We also recovered several items believed to belong to the victims," he said, adding that the suspect has been remanded for a week beginning today.
The case is being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years, and not fewer than 12 strokes of the cane if the death penalty is not imposed.
It is also being investigated under Section 394 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt while committing robbery, which carries a maximum jail term of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
Police urged witnesses or anyone with information related to the incident to contact the Manjung district police headquarters operations room at 05-688 6222 or the WhatsApp hotline at 017-682 8005 to assist in investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang exco says no misappropriation in RM500,000 fishermen voucher programme, cites document delays
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, May 8 — Penang state exco Datuk Rashidi Zinol has denied any misappropriation following claims by fishing equipment shop operators that they had not received payments under a RM500,000 fishermen voucher programme.
The Rural Development, Agrotechnology, Food Security and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman said there were issues with payments involving various agencies, namely the Malaysian Fisheries Development Authority (LKIM), the State Economic Planning Division (BPEN) and several state financial agencies.
“Checks found there were documentation issues as some company documents were incomplete, which delayed the payment process to the eight companies involved,” he explained during a press conference today.
He said six of the eight companies had submitted complete documents and would be eligible for payment first.
“The remaining two companies have yet to complete their documents and we have informed them to do so,” he said.
He was commenting on a police report lodged by fishing equipment shop operators today claiming they had not received payments from the fishermen voucher programme, which is under the state exco office.
According to Rashidi, the funds from LKIM had been returned to the State Economic Planning Division before the repayment process was reorganised according to procedure.
“If there was misappropriation, the money would not have been easily returned,” he said.
He said it was clear that this was a technical and documentation issue.
Rashidi also denied allegations that BPEN had not responded to enquiries from the companies involved, explaining that the review and payment process was being coordinated in stages.
“We are now expediting the process and payments will be channelled by next week at the latest once all documents are finalised,” he said.
He reminded the affected companies to submit completed documents to receive the payments.
“Payment will be made next week,” he asaid.
He said it was the companies’ right to lodge police reports on the issue to seek clarification.
“If they want to lodge a police report, go ahead, we have no problem because we are confident there was no misappropriation. Let the police investigate,” he said.

Title: Penang yet to decide on RM1b Karpal Singh Drive project as developer seeks extension, says state exco
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, May 8 — The fate of the proposed Karpal Singh Drive reclamation project remains undecided, with the developer seeking more time to revise and resubmit its plans.
The developer, PLB Engineering Berhad, will be applying to project owner, Penang Development Corporation (PDC), for an extension of time (EOT), state local government committee chairman Jason H’ng Mooi Lye said in a news conference at the State Legislative Assembly building here today.
“PDC will then present the working papers in relation to the EOT to the state exco on May 20,” he said, adding that any decision on whether the RM1 billion project proceeds or is scrapped will only be made after that review.
H’ng said the state is still evaluating the project following repeated setbacks in its environmental approval process.
He confirmed that the Department of Environment (DoE) had rejected the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) application for the fifth time in March.
“For now, we have not decided to proceed or to scrap the project, we need to look at the overall project and decide based on various factors that will be presented to us on May 20,” he said.
He said discussions with PDC and PLB Engineering will focus on whether the revised conditions set by regulators can be met.
“The developer will be updating the conditions set by DoE and at the same time, we will look at whether the conditions can be met,” he said.
PLB Engineering has also proposed changes to the project scope, including adding a public recreational park component.
H’ng said the DoE had rejected the application on March 2 after technical details were found to be insufficient.
The reclamation plan involves about 30 hectares off Karpal Singh Drive as part of a wider redevelopment of the Jelutong landfill area.
The project, first agreed in 2020 between PDC and PLB Engineering, has drawn repeated objections from environmental groups over its proximity to the Middle Bank marine sanctuary, which is recognised as a biodiversity-rich seagrass ecosystem in the Strait of Malacca.

Title: Van driver claims trial to reckless driving charge over death of Year Two pupil in Batu Pahat
Article:
BATU PAHAT, May 8 — A van driver pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to reckless driving resulting in the death of an eight-year-old girl last Tuesday.
Nordin Ithnin, 61, entered the plea before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass.
According to the charge, the accused allegedly drove a white van recklessly, causing the death of Nur Aini Umairah Mohd Fareez, who was walking along the road in front of Sekolah Kebangsaan Seri Maimon, Parit Sulong, at about 12.30pm on May 5.
The charge, framed under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, provides for a maximum prison sentence of 10 years and a fine of up to RM50,000 upon conviction.
The court allowed the accused bail of RM5,000 with one surety and ordered him to report to a nearby police station once a month.
The court also ordered the suspension of the accused’s driving licence effective from the date of the charge and fixed June 25 for case mention and submission of documents
It was earlier reported that the Year Two pupil died after being struck by a van in front of the main gate of SK Seri Maimon, Parit Sulong, while walking home.
Initial investigations found the van was leaving the school compound after delivering goods when the incident occurred.
The victim sustained severe head and internal injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene. — Bernama

Title: No ‘tebuk atap’ in Negeri Sembilan, says Mat Sabu as Amanah backs PKR’s Aminuddin as MB
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu today dismissed speculation that the party’s sole assemblyman in Negeri Sembilan could be elevated to the menteri besar post.
He affirmed that Amanah remains aligned with the current state leadership and is not involved in any move to replace Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun who is from PKR.
“No way Amanah will lead the ‘tebuk atap’ in Negeri Sembilan. The menteri besar Negeri Sembilan is currently Datuk Aminuddin. There will be no other menteri besar apart from PKR, from Pakatan Harapan apart from Datuk Aminuddin.
“And Amanah’s sole elected representative in Negeri Sembilan must support Datuk Aminuddin. Full stop,” Mohamad, who is also better known as Mat Sabu, said in a Facebook reel posted this morning.
He stressed that Amanah rejects any attempt to form a government through backdoor manoeuvres or as he said in Malay ‘tebuk atap’, describing such efforts as contrary to the party’s principles.
Amanah’s position, he added, is to support cooperation within the unity government framework and focus on delivering for Negeri Sembilan residents.

Title: Police: Woman crashes car into Tumpat laundry shop after pressing wrong pedal
Article:
KOTA BHARU, May 8 — A woman is believed to have accidentally pressed the accelerator while making a U-turn which caused the car to crash into a laundry shop at Pasar Besar Wakaf Bharu, Tumpat, yesterday.
Tumpat district police chief ACP Mohd Khairi Shafie said the 11.20am incident involved a Honda City driven by a local woman in her 50s.
He said initial investigations found that the woman was driving from Jalan Puteri Saadong towards the laundry premises before making a U-turn at the scene of the incident.
“However, it is believed that the driver involved accidentally pressed the accelerator, causing the vehicle she was driving to swerve and crash into the glass door of the laundry store.
“As a result of the incident, the glass door of the premises was broken but no injuries were reported,” he said in a statement today.
Mohd Khairi said his party received a report on the incident at about 12.40pm from the 32-year-old owner of the laundry shop.
He said the case was being investigated in accordance with Regulation 10, Traffic Rules 1959 and members of the public with information regarding the incident were asked to contact the Tumpat district police headquarters (IPD) at 09-7257222 to assist in the investigation. — Bernama

Title: Malaysia to roll out MyNIISe to reduce congestion at Johor-Singapore checkpoint from 2027
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 8 — A new digital immigration system aimed at easing one of the world’s busiest land borders will be introduced at the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said today.
He said the National Integrated Immigration System (MyNIISe) will be deployed at the Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex at Bukit Chagar when it begins operations on January 1, 2027.
He said the system’s full rollout will coincide with the launch of the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link service, which is expected to significantly improve cross-border connectivity between Johor and Singapore.
